conjectures.title: Caching reduces p99 latency by 50%
conjectures.hypothesis: Adding Redis cache for DB queries will cut tail latency in half
evidence: TLA+ maps well: 5 states (open/testing/confirmed/refuted/abandoned), guards as ENABLED conditions
Key invariants: confirmed requires hypothesis+2 evidence, refuted requires 1 evidence, abandoned always reachable

conjectures.title: Dark mode increases engagement
conjectures.hypothesis: Users with dark mode enabled have 10% longer sessions
evidence: A/B test setup with 5000 users per cohort

conjectures.title: Null hypothesis: button color doesn't matter
conjectures.hypothesis: Red vs blue CTA button has no significant conversion difference
evidence: conversion: red 3.2%, blue 3.1%

conjectures.title: State machine is intuitive and passes TLA+ specifications
conjectures.hypothesis: Guards and transitions can be formally verified with TLA+
evidence: TLA+ maps well: 5 states (open/testing/confirmed/refuted/abandoned), guards as ENABLED conditions
Key invariants: confirmed requires hypothesis+2 evidence, refuted requires 1 evidence, abandoned always reachable

conjectures.title: cprr fills a unique niche not covered by claude-code-tools
conjectures.hypothesis: claude-code-tools focuses on session/env management, not hypothesis tracking with formal guards
evidence: claude-code-tools contains: aichat (session mgmt), tmux-cli (terminal automation), vault (env backup), env-safe (env inspection) - no hypothesis/experiment tracking
cprr provides: state machine with guards, hypothesis->testing->confirmed workflow, evidence requirements - complementary not overlapping

conjectures.title: cprr methodology aligns with multi-agent debate workflows (Mysti HN discussion)
conjectures.hypothesis: Conjecture-refutation cycle mirrors how multiple agents challenge and synthesize solutions
evidence: Mysti (HN 46365105): multi-agent debate where models challenge each other's approaches - analogous to conjecture/refutation cycle
Key insight from discussion: 'if a model introduces a bug, it has hard time spotting it, but another model can instantly spot it' - supports systematic hypothesis testing across perspectives

conjectures.title: cprr can be packaged as an Agent Skills skill
conjectures.hypothesis: SKILL.md + scripts/ structure maps to cprr's CLI and methodology
evidence: SKILL.md created with: overview, workflow, commands, evidence standards, best practices, example session - follows agent-skills pattern
Structure maps: SKILL.md (instructions) exists, cprr CLI provides scripts/ equivalent via make targets, docs/ contains references/ - minimal viable skill package complete

conjectures.title: Alloy is better than TLA+ for cprr's small state machines
conjectures.hypothesis: Alloy's relational logic and built-in visualization provide faster iteration for bounded state spaces like cprr's 5-state machine
evidence: Alloy advantages for cprr: (1) relational model fits guard predicates naturally, (2) Analyzer generates counterexamples visually, (3) bounded checking sufficient for 5 states × small evidence counts, (4) no temporal operators needed - guards are structural not temporal

TLA+ overhead for cprr: (1) temporal logic ([]/<>) overkill for simple guards, (2) TLC model checker designed for distributed systems concurrency, (3) no built-in visualization, (4) steeper learning curve for structural properties
Alloy spec (cprr.als) is 140 lines: guards as predicates, transitions as relations, invariants as assertions with 'check' - directly maps to cprr's domain model without temporal logic overhead
Alloy spec validated: PROOF.md documents manual review and verification steps

conjectures.title: Vale is better than custom linters for experiment content validation
conjectures.hypothesis: Vale's prose linting + custom vocabulary catches more style issues than bash grep checks, with less maintenance burden

conjectures.title: Refinery pattern simplifies multi-agent merge conflicts
conjectures.hypothesis: beads and cprr could use append-only logs with periodic refinement/compaction to avoid merge conflicts when multiple agents work in parallel

conjectures.title: Branches and worktrees prevent duplicate experiment numbers
conjectures.hypothesis: When agents use isolated branches/worktrees, experiment number collisions are caught at merge time and trivially resolved by renumbering

conjectures.title: Conjectures should be project-local by default, not global
conjectures.hypothesis: Project-specific hypotheses (experiments/, .beads/) should live in .cprr/ not ~/.cprr/ - global store causes sync issues and mixes unrelated projects

conjectures.title: cprr should use bead semantics for branch-style isolation
conjectures.hypothesis: When cprr isn't core to a repo, it should support branch-like isolation (like beads) rather than a single global/local store - enables ephemeral hypothesis tracking without polluting the main project

conjectures.title: cprr has a coherent multi-agent collaboration story
conjectures.hypothesis: With worktrees, branch isolation, local-first stores, and git merge semantics, cprr supports parallel work across machines/agents with tractable integration
evidence: Demonstrated today: two agents on different machines (001-quickstart-command vs 002-alloy-state-machine) worked in parallel, merged cleanly, experiments indexed in README.org
Components: .cprr/ in git, worktrees/ gitignored, experiments/NNN structure, .beads/ tracking, lint-experiments.sh validation

conjectures.title: Documentation and quickstart are aligned on CLI testing matrix
conjectures.hypothesis: docs/CLI-TESTING.org, SKILL.md, and cprr quickstart output consistently show flag conflicts (-h), parameter requirements, and state transitions
evidence: Inconsistency found: CONTRIBUTING.org line 90 shows '-h' for hypothesis, but quickstart and main.go:513 correctly show '--hypothesis' to avoid conflict with help flag
Fixed: Updated CONTRIBUTING.org and main.go to consistently use --hypothesis (not -h) - aligns with quickstart and CLI-TESTING.org

conjectures.title: cprr quickstart idempotently updates AGENTS.md
conjectures.hypothesis: Running cprr quickstart multiple times safely adds CPRR methodology section to AGENTS.md without duplication, enabling adoption in any repo

conjectures.title: cprr and bd init/quickstart should be idempotent like bd install/uninstall
conjectures.hypothesis: Running cprr init, cprr quickstart, bd init, bd install multiple times produces same result - no duplicate entries, no errors, safe re-runs
evidence: Current state: cprr init errors if exists (exit 1), bd init errors if exists (exit 1), cprr quickstart creates new demo conjecture each run (not idempotent - accumulates #16, #17, #18...)
Implemented: cprr now uses local-first detection, doctor command provides migration and health checks

conjectures.title: cprr doctor command for migrations and health checks
conjectures.hypothesis: A doctor command can validate store integrity, migrate schema versions, and fix common issues - similar to bd doctor, brew doctor, npm doctor patterns
evidence: Use cases: (1) schema migration when conjecture fields change, (2) orphaned evidence cleanup, (3) validate state machine invariants, (4) repair global/local sync issues
Prior art: bd install docs show manual AGENTS.md update (echo >> AGENTS.md) - cprr could follow same pattern or offer cprr doctor --update-agents

conjectures.title: Quickstart demo conjecture
conjectures.hypothesis: This demo will complete in <5 seconds
evidence: Demo evidence 1: command executed
Demo evidence 2: output rendered
Demo evidence 1: command executed
Demo evidence 2: output rendered

conjectures.title: Core docs, testing docs, and implementation are aligned
conjectures.hypothesis: README, CONTRIBUTING, CLI-TESTING, SKILL.md all show consistent flags and behaviors matching main.go implementation
evidence: Updated: README.org, CONTRIBUTING.org, CLI-TESTING.org all use 'cprr init --examples' (local default), init tests show exit 0 for idempotent behavior
Verified: main.go cmdInit defaults useLocal=true, help shows --global as opt-in, quickstart is idempotent (skips existing demo)

conjectures.title: cprr honors --global selection from initialization
conjectures.hypothesis: If initialized with --global, subsequent cprr commands use ~/.cprr unless local .cprr/ exists (local-first precedence)
evidence: Verified: --global creates ~/.cprr, subsequent commands use it when no local .cprr/ exists. Local-first: if .cprr/ exists in pwd, it takes precedence regardless of global init
cprr doctor shows both stores with (ACTIVE) marker on whichever is in use - user can see which store is active and why

conjectures.title: Global vs per-project semantics are well-understood and cascade correctly
conjectures.hypothesis: cprr has clear precedence rules: pwd .cprr/ > ~/.cprr, documented in doctor output, init help, and README
evidence: Precedence: (1) .cprr/conjectures.json in pwd checked first, (2) ~/.cprr/conjectures.json fallback. Doctor shows which is ACTIVE. No merge/cascade of data - winner takes all.
Semantics documented: init --help shows local default, doctor explains stores, loadStore() line 144-146 implements detection. No config inheritance - each store is independent.

conjectures.title: worktrees/ directory should be gitignored for multi-machine isolation
conjectures.hypothesis: Adding worktrees/ to .gitignore ensures each machine can have independent worktrees without conflicts - agents on different machines won't see or interfere with each other's parallel branch checkouts
evidence: .gitignore:61 already contains 'worktrees/'
git check-ignore confirms worktrees/ is ignored (exit 0)

conjectures.title: External agents detect in-flight work via branches and worktrees/
conjectures.hypothesis: When agents clone/pull repo, they can see WIP branches and optionally check them out to worktrees/ for parallel work without conflicts
evidence: Demonstrated: spawned agent to work on CPRR-fob in feat/CPRR-fob-alloy-validation branch - work isolated, pushes to origin for other agents to discover
Discovery mechanism: git fetch --all shows remote branches, agents can git worktree add ../cprr-CPRR-fob origin/feat/CPRR-fob-alloy-validation to work in parallel without checkout conflicts
Correction: worktrees/ is gitignored in pwd, so git worktree add worktrees/CPRR-fob origin/feat/... keeps isolation local while branches are shared via origin
